jokes.text: I don't shake hands. I have avian bone syndrome. Hollow bones.
jokes.setup: Previous episode recap showing character refusing handshake
jokes.punchline: Ridiculously specific fake medical condition with hollow bones like a bird
jokes.explanation: Brilliant absurdist medical excuse that's both specific and ridiculous. Perfect economy and character-specific neurosis.

jokes.text: Blurg.
jokes.punchline: Liz's signature nonsense word as emotional expression
jokes.explanation: Peak character integration and economy. One made-up word that perfectly captures Liz's emotional state.

jokes.text: Do you... have any questions about the wedding night?
jokes.punchline: Inappropriate personal question from doctor
jokes.explanation: Standard awkward doctor joke. Functional but predictable setup.

jokes.text: 310/280. We have a winner.
jokes.punchline: Impossibly high blood pressure treated as game show victory
jokes.explanation: Solid dark comedy with medical absurdity. Good economy and unexpected game show framing.

jokes.text: My boyfriend is moving to Cleveland, but I'm gonna go visit him over vacation. And my boss is super mad at me because I know that he fell asleep on top of his fiancée.
jokes.punchline: Escalating overshare ending with bizarre workplace knowledge
jokes.explanation: Perfect escalation structure and character oversharing. Builds from normal to absurd personal revelations.

jokes.text: My techniques guarantee male orgasm.
jokes.punchline: Wildly inappropriate professional guarantee
jokes.explanation: Shocking inappropriateness from medical professional. High impact but low character integration.

jokes.text: And it's the season finale of my show this week, and the star is missing and may have been abducted by a cabal of powerful black celebrities.
jokes.punchline: Completely absurd conspiracy theory stated matter-of-factly
jokes.explanation: Brilliant absurdist escalation. 'Cabal of powerful black celebrities' is perfectly specific and ridiculous.

jokes.text: Can I play Barack Obama? No. It's bad enough we have Tracy playing Barack Obama.
jokes.setup: Josh volunteering to take Tracy's parts
jokes.punchline: Liz's brutal honesty about Tracy's inappropriate casting
jokes.explanation: Sharp workplace comedy with meta commentary on casting choices. Good economy and timing.

jokes.text: Should I include Tracy and his guests, or do you think he's dead?
jokes.punchline: Casual consideration of death as party planning factor
jokes.explanation: Perfect dark workplace comedy. Treats death as mundane logistical concern.

jokes.text: We used to do it every week without him. I'm just saying.
jokes.setup: Liz saying they need Tracy for the show
jokes.punchline: Josh's passive-aggressive reminder of Tracy's attendance record
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Josh's workplace resentment. Understated delivery works well.

jokes.text: I will cut you open like a tauntaun... you mouth-breathing Appalachian!
jokes.punchline: Star Wars reference combined with regional insult
jokes.explanation: Perfect Jack Donaghy escalation. Combines pop culture with elitist insults in character-specific way.

jokes.text: My name is Gordon Tremeshko.
jokes.punchline: Comically fake Eastern European alias
jokes.explanation: Solid absurd alias that sounds appropriately fake. Good character moment for Tracy's poor planning.

jokes.text: I'm Jesse Parcell, Kenneth's cousin.
jokes.punchline: Obvious family resemblance through shared innocent demeanor
jokes.explanation: Character setup that pays off Kenneth's established rural background. Functional but not exceptional.

jokes.text: Shh! It really riles up the wolves.
jokes.setup: Jesse explaining why to be quiet at night
jokes.punchline: Casual mention of dangerous wildlife as normal concern
jokes.explanation: Excellent escalation of rural dangers. Casual delivery makes wolves seem like minor inconvenience.

jokes.text: I nearly wet my other pair of underpants.
jokes.setup: Jesse describing reaction to Tracy's movie
jokes.punchline: Implication he only owns two pairs of underwear
jokes.explanation: Perfect character detail revealing poverty through casual mention. Great economy and specificity.

jokes.text: She's got a good, solid baby bucket.
jokes.punchline: Crudely clinical assessment of reproductive capability
jokes.explanation: Brilliantly crude and specific phrase that perfectly captures Colleen's blunt personality. Maximum impact per word.

jokes.text: You are a sassy old broad, aren't you?
jokes.setup: Liz responding to Colleen's outrageous behavior
jokes.punchline: Direct confrontation disguised as compliment
jokes.explanation: Good Liz moment showing her directness. Not groundbreaking but fits character well.

jokes.text: Rooster, this is the farmer. Is the egg safe? The egg is in the nest.
jokes.punchline: Overly elaborate spy code from simple rural characters
jokes.explanation: Good absurdist moment showing Kenneth's elaborate planning. Farm metaphors fit character background perfectly.

jokes.text: Did he go to Russell Simmons' birthday party? Were there girls there with butts?
jokes.setup: Tracy asking about New York social scene
jokes.punchline: Escalation from legitimate question to crude obsession
jokes.explanation: Perfect Tracy Jordan priorities. Shows his shallow celebrity obsessions and crude humor.

jokes.text: You're name's Gordon Tremeshko. You live in Needmore, Pennsylvania, where we don't go out at night 'cause of the wolves.
jokes.punchline: Callback to wolves as serious local concern
jokes.explanation: Good callback that reinforces the rural setting's absurd dangers. Works as character consistency.

jokes.text: Um... it's actually in the mid-40s. Oh, really? 'Cause it's low 40s here.
jokes.setup: Liz trying to make conversation about weather
jokes.punchline: Painfully mundane temperature comparison revealing relationship problems
jokes.explanation: Perfect cringe comedy showing relationship strain through mundane conversation. Painfully realistic.

jokes.text: I want two lobsters... totaling five pounds of lobster meat.
jokes.setup: Jack asking Liz for a favor
jokes.punchline: Liz's very specific and greedy demands
jokes.explanation: Perfect Liz food obsession with specific weight requirements. Shows her practical but gluttonous nature.

jokes.text: And after that, we can clean the dead birds out the chimney. That's why we bought these metal rods. Don't you just love spring?
jokes.punchline: Grisly chore presented as cheerful seasonal tradition
jokes.explanation: Excellent dark comedy juxtaposing death with seasonal joy. Perfect rural character moment.

jokes.text: I'll pretty much do anything to someone that's famous.
jokes.setup: Woman bragging about encounter with DJ
jokes.punchline: Blunt admission of celebrity obsession
jokes.explanation: Direct and crude character moment. Good economy but not particularly clever or original.

jokes.text: She's like a white geisha.
jokes.punchline: Bizarrely specific and inappropriate cultural comparison
jokes.explanation: Perfectly inappropriate Colleen observation. Combines casual racism with bizarre specificity.

jokes.text: And it's not 'Bee-yan-ka,' like Sanka. It's 'Bee-yon-ka,' like Willy Won... Wonka!
jokes.punchline: Elaborate pronunciation guide that falls apart
jokes.explanation: Brilliant structure where the mnemonic device breaks down mid-explanation. Perfect Colleen moment showing her pretentiousness.

jokes.text: I'd rather die famous than to live for 100 years like this, carrying plastic bags, sitting on benches, brushing my own teeth.
jokes.setup: Tracy explaining why he wants to return
jokes.punchline: Escalating list ending with basic hygiene as ultimate hardship
jokes.explanation: Perfect Tracy Jordan escalation showing his complete detachment from normal life. Brushing teeth as ultimate hardship is brilliant.

jokes.text: It's like wearing your own skin to bed.
jokes.punchline: Disturbing metaphor for luxury bedwear
jokes.explanation: Effectively disturbing imagery that makes luxury sound creepy. Good economy and impact.

jokes.text: Careful... my bones.
jokes.punchline: Callback to hollow bone syndrome during action
jokes.explanation: Excellent callback that pays off episode opening. Perfect integration of absurd medical condition with action.

jokes.text: Oh, God, this is it. Here it comes... the big one. Ride it, Donaghy. Ride it straight to hell!
jokes.punchline: Dramatic internal monologue during medical emergency
jokes.explanation: Good Jack moment showing his dramatic internal life. Self-motivation during heart attack is character-appropriate.

jokes.text: Ugh! Is that how far apart my eyes are? I look like Admiral Ackbar.
jokes.setup: Liz seeing herself on video call
jokes.punchline: Star Wars character comparison for eye spacing
jokes.explanation: Perfect Liz self-deprecation with specific pop culture reference. Good character consistency with insecurity.

jokes.text: I was at a costume party earlier this evening. And the hostess's dog attacked me, so I had to stab it.
jokes.setup: Colleen explaining her appearance at hospital
jokes.punchline: Casual admission of stabbing dog in self-defense
jokes.explanation: Brilliant dark comedy escalation. Colleen's matter-of-fact delivery of shocking violence is perfect character work.

jokes.text: Also, I suspect he may have scurvy because he keeps asking for lemon. Ah. No. That's me.
jokes.punchline: Misdiagnosis based on patient calling for Liz Lemon
jokes.explanation: Excellent misdirection joke with perfect setup and payoff. Medical setting makes scurvy diagnosis plausible.

jokes.text: Tell him his mother's here. And she loves him. But not in a queer way.
jokes.setup: Colleen wanting to send message to Jack
jokes.punchline: Unnecessary clarification that makes it more inappropriate
jokes.explanation: Perfect Colleen moment where clarification makes things worse. Character-consistent inappropriate behavior.

jokes.text: I should have worked more.
jokes.setup: Jack sharing his one life regret
jokes.punchline: Workaholic's impossible regret about working too little
jokes.explanation: Brilliant character moment. Perfect economy and irony - only Jack would regret not working more after heart attack.

jokes.text: Maybe this is the drugs talking, but I think I got Nixon to agree to come on the show and say, 'Sock it to me.'
jokes.setup: Jack making business calls from hospital bed
jokes.punchline: Delusional booking of dead president for dated TV reference
jokes.explanation: Excellent absurdist moment combining drug effects with Jack's delusions of business power. Multiple layers of impossibility.

jokes.text: I'd invite you in, but I got a living room full of dead chimney birds.
jokes.setup: Jesse explaining why Kenneth can't come inside
jokes.punchline: Callback to earlier mentioned chimney cleaning project
jokes.explanation: Good callback that pays off earlier setup. Casual treatment of grisly situation works well.

jokes.text: We must rest, my chimney bird. Tonight we're reading my screenplay.
jokes.punchline: Treating corpse as entertainment companion
jokes.explanation: Excellent escalation of rural isolation. Treating dead bird as audience for creative work is perfectly disturbing.

jokes.text: Your move, creep.
jokes.setup: Josh suggesting he do RoboCop impression
jokes.punchline: Outdated movie reference as contemporary entertainment
jokes.explanation: Weak comedy showing Josh's desperation and poor taste. Commentary on bad TV filler but not particularly funny.

jokes.text: By the hammer of Thor! You can't get away from me! I'm gonna find you! I know what you look like! I remember what you look like! You're so stupid. I got to reload.
jokes.punchline: Escalating threats ending with tactical incompetence
jokes.explanation: Good escalation showing Jesse's rural incompetence. Thor reference plus reload problem creates character comedy.

jokes.text: Yeah, the President's in town, there's a transit strike, it's the Dominican Independence Day parade, a guy in suspenders is doing chalk paintings down on Canal Street.
jokes.punchline: List escalating from serious to random street performer
jokes.explanation: Good escalation from legitimate to absurd causes. Chalk painting detail is perfect random NYC specificity.

jokes.text: Oh, my God, what a terrible mistake accident!
jokes.setup: Kenneth causing accident to get ambulance ride
jokes.punchline: Overly elaborate fake surprise at planned incident
jokes.explanation: Good Kenneth moment showing his innocent scheming. Verbal stumble makes fake surprise obvious.

jokes.text: If I die, will you take care of my birds? I got a lot on my plate right now, Ken. Oh. Tweety.
jokes.punchline: Tracy's dismissal followed by Kenneth's resigned single bird name
jokes.explanation: Perfect character dynamic. Kenneth's simple 'Tweety' after rejection shows his gentle sadness.

jokes.text: You're the only person I know who wouldn't hesitate to pull the plug.
jokes.setup: Liz asking about being emergency contact
jokes.punchline: Jack's morbid reasoning for choosing Liz
jokes.explanation: Brilliant dark comedy showing twisted but logical friendship dynamic. Perfect Jack reasoning.

jokes.text: I no longer think you're doing a terrible job, and I'm very proud of you.
jokes.setup: Jack giving Liz feedback on the show
jokes.punchline: Backhanded compliment starting with previous low opinion
jokes.explanation: Perfect Jack communication style. Manages to insult while complimenting in character-appropriate way.

jokes.text: I'm gonna pull the plug now. Whoa, whoa. Just let me do it.
jokes.punchline: Jack's control-freak need to manage own death
jokes.explanation: Perfect callback and character moment. Jack needing control even over his own death is brilliant.
